findings.claim: The file src/storage/schema.ts introduces a new persistence artifact — an `orders` table schema with a defined column structure (integer primary key, a text payload column, and a text created_at column) — with no accompanying architecture decision record explaining the choice of this table shape, its column typing (notably storing `payload` as unstructured text rather than a normalized/typed structure), or how it fits the system's persistence boundary.
findings.consequence: Without a recorded rationale, a future maintainer will not know why orders are persisted with this exact schema (e.g., why payload is stored as opaque text rather than structured columns, whether this is meant to be append-only, or what constraints/indexes were intentionally omitted), which risks incompatible migrations or misinformed schema changes down the line.

findings.claim: package.json establishes direct runtime dependencies on axios (HTTP client) and yaml (YAML parser) with no decision record explaining the choice of these specific libraries or the architectural boundary they introduce (external HTTP calls, YAML-based parsing/config).
findings.consequence: If either dependency was newly introduced by this change, a maintainer six months out will lack the reasoning for why this HTTP client or serialization format was chosen over alternatives (e.g., native fetch, JSON-based config), making future replacement or auditing of the dependency harder to justify.
